sentences.english: Today we have Jenny Park with us, the owner of Olive & Grain, a bakery and café right here in downtown Portland. Jenny, thanks for joining us.
sentences.korean: 오늘 올리브 앤 그레인의 사장 제니 파크와 함께합니다. 포틀랜드 시내에 있는 베이커리 겸 카페예요.

sentences.english: Thanks for having me. I'm excited to be here.
sentences.korean: 초대해주셔서 감사해요. 여기 오게 되어 기뻐요.

sentences.english: So you opened Olive & Grain about three years ago. What made you take the leap into starting your own business?
sentences.korean: 올리브 앤 그레인을 약 3년 전에 오픈하셨죠. 자기 사업을 시작하게 된 계기가 뭔가요?

sentences.english: Honestly, it was a combination of passion and frustration. I'd been working as a pastry chef at a hotel for about eight years, and while I loved the craft, I didn't love the corporate environment.
sentences.korean: 솔직히 열정과 답답함이 합쳐진 거였어요. 호텔에서 페이스트리 셰프로 약 8년간 일했거든요.

sentences.english: I wanted to create a space where everything — from the ingredients to the atmosphere — reflected my values.
sentences.korean: 재료부터 분위기까지 모든 것이 제 가치관을 반영하는 공간을 만들고 싶었어요.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: Economic Analysis — The Paradox of Thrift

sentences.english: I wanted to use local, organic ingredients and build a place where people felt like they were walking into someone's home.
sentences.korean: 지역 유기농 재료를 사용하고, 사람들이 누군가의 집에 들어온 것 같은 느낌의 공간을 만들고 싶었어요.

sentences.english: And you've clearly succeeded. The reviews are incredible. But I imagine the first year was tough?
sentences.korean: 분명히 성공하셨네요. 리뷰가 정말 좋더라고요. 하지만 첫해는 힘들었을 것 같은데요?

sentences.english: Brutal, honestly. I underestimated how much of running a bakery is not baking. It's managing finances, hiring, dealing with suppliers, marketing — all things I had zero experience in.
sentences.korean: 솔직히 잔인했어요. 빵집 운영의 대부분이 빵 굽는 게 아니라는 걸 과소평가했어요. 재무 관리, 채용, 공급업체 처리거든요.

sentences.english: There were months where I barely broke even. I almost closed the doors after the first winter.
sentences.korean: 겨우 손익분기점을 맞추는 달도 있었고, 첫 겨울 후에 거의 문을 닫을 뻔했어요.

sentences.english: My regulars. By that point, I had maybe twenty or thirty loyal customers who came in every single week. Some of them wrote me letters when they heard I was struggling. That community support was what kept me afloat.
sentences.korean: 단골들이요. 그때쯤 매주 빠짐없이 오는 2~30명의 충성 고객이 있었어요.

sentences.english: That's beautiful. What's next for Olive & Grain?
sentences.korean: 아름답네요. 올리브 앤 그레인의 다음 계획은 뭔가요?

sentences.english: We're launching a weekend brunch menu next month, and I'm exploring the idea of a small cooking class series. Nothing too ambitious — I've learned that lesson.
sentences.korean: 다음 달에 주말 브런치 메뉴를 출시하고, 소규모 요리 교실 시리즈도 구상 중이에요.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: Panel Discussion — AI and the Future of Work
